Rue Vinâve d'Île pedestrian street with the Fontaine de la Vierge
La Batte Sunday market along the Meuse
Hamad Town Grand Mosque, a sand-coloured mosque with a tall slender minaret and arched lattice windows
Dilmun burial mounds of Hamad Town
Hamad Town Park, a simple residential neighbourhood garden with date palms, green lawns, low hedges and a paved walking path, with low white Gulf villas in the background, no monuments or towers
Hamad Town shopping street with modern low-rise concrete shop buildings, awnings over fruit and vegetable displays, and parked-free clean sidewalks, 1980s planned Gulf town style
Metropolitan Cathedral of Sucre with its white bell tower
Parque Simón Bolívar with its miniature Eiffel Tower
Plaza 25 de Mayo, the main square of Sucre lined with white colonial arcades, palm trees and garden beds
Mercado Central of Sucre, a white two-storey covered market building with arched entrances and fruit stalls spilling onto the sidewalk
Cathedral of Assis seen from the palm-lined Praça da Catedral
Parque Buracão with its tree-lined walking path and wooden suspension footbridge
Avenida Rui Barbosa, the main shopping avenue of Assis lined with low-rise storefronts, awnings and mid-rise apartment blocks
Teatro Municipal de Assis, a salmon-pink art deco theatre with a crenellated round corner tower
Igreja Matriz de São João Batista and its landscaped church square
Orla de Cametá, the riverfront promenade along the Tocantins River with its blue-and-white balustrade
Avenida Gentil Bittencourt, a commercial avenue of small one- and two-storey painted shopfronts with awnings under leafy mango trees, typical of an Amazonian river town
The Historic Museum of Cametá, a modest single-storey white palacete with blue trim, a triangular gable crowned by slender pyramid pinnacles and three arched pedimented doorways on a quiet corner
Igreja Matriz Nossa Senhora do Monte Serrat, the yellow-and-white parish church overlooking its square
Praça Joaquim Nunes, the central town square paved with colorful painted mosaic patterns under large shade trees
Rua Batista Cepelos, a central shopping street of low whitewashed shopfronts with blue awnings and black-and-white patterned Portuguese pavement sidewalks
The Paço Municipal of Cotia, a contemporary glass-and-concrete civic building fronted by a landscaped flag plaza and a sculpted concrete relief wall
